scaled_oracle_cases.description: Normal case with all attributes present, verifying exact output format and contents per C-axis contract.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must produce the exact version message with all components (Macsyfinder version, Python, NetworkX, Pandas, citation) under normal module setup.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Output must include all required lines in the exact order and format as specified in the seed case, with no missing or extra lines.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: solution that omits a required section, changes formatting, or crashes under normal conditions.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new requirement introduced by this operator.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Ensure function does not crash when __citation__ is missing from macsypy module and returns a valid version string.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function should gracefully handle the absence of __citation__ attribute by either omitting the citation line or using a default message, without raising an exception.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When macsypy.__citation__ is not present, the function must not raise an AttributeError and must return a string that includes at least the version and the other required sections (Python, NetworkX, Pandas) but may exclude or modify the citation line.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Function that directly accesses macsypy.__citation__ without using getattr or try/except will crash with AttributeError.
covered_requirements: The function must handle missing optional attributes gracefully.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Ensure function does not crash when solution attribute is missing from macsypy module and returns a valid version string possibly without NetworkX version.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function should gracefully handle the absence of solution attribute (hence no NetworkX version) without raising an exception, and should still include Macsyfinder, Python, Pandas versions.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When macsypy.solution is not present, the function must not crash and must return a string that includes at least the Macsyfinder version and Python version, but may omit or handle the NetworkX line gracefully.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: If function unconditionally accesses macsypy.solution.nx.__version__, it will crash with AttributeError.
covered_requirements: The function must handle missing optional attributes gracefully.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Ensure function handles missing both __citation__ and solution attributes, producing a version string without crash and with basic version info.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must jointly handle the absence of both optional attributes while still producing a coherent version message that includes Macsyfinder version and Python version.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When both __citation__ and solution are missing, the function must not crash and must return a string that includes Macsyfinder version and Python version, but may omit or modify the NetworkX and citation lines.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Function that fails to handle either missing attribute causes crash or produces incomplete output.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
The function must handle missing optional attributes gracefully.
